Amendments1994— amended section generally, substituting administrative provisions for provisions relating to compensation of members of Commission.Pub. L. 103–419

children.label: A
children.text: serve as the administrative head of the Commission; and

children.label: B
children.text: be appointed by the President with the concurrence of a majority of the Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: appoint such other personnel as it deems advisable, under the civil service and classification laws; and

children.label: B
children.text: procure services, as authorized in , but at rates for individuals not in excess of the daily equivalent paid for positions at the maximum rate for GS–15 of the General Schedule under .section 3109 of title 5section 5332 of title 5

children.heading: Generally
children.label: 1 Generally
children.text: Each member of the Commission who is not otherwise in the service of the Government of the United States shall receive a sum equivalent to the compensation paid at level IV of the Executive Schedule under , prorated on a daily basis for time spent in the work of the Commission.section 5315 of title 5

children.heading: Persons otherwise in Government service
children.label: 2 Persons otherwise in Government service
children.text: Each member of the Commission who is otherwise in the service of the Government of the United States shall serve without compensation in addition to that received for such other service, but while engaged in the work of the Commission shall be paid actual travel expenses and per diem in lieu of subsistence expenses when away from such member’s usual place of residence, under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5.

children.heading: Voluntary or uncompensated personnel
children.label: c Voluntary or uncompensated personnel
children.text: The Commission shall not accept or use the services of voluntary or uncompensated persons. This limitation shall apply with respect to services of members of the Commission as it does with respect to services by other persons.

children.heading: Generally
children.label: 1 Generally
children.text: The Commission may make such rules as are necessary to carry out the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: Continuation of old rules
children.label: 2 Continuation of old rules
children.text: Except as inconsistent with this chapter, and until modified by the Commission, the rules of the Commission on Civil Rights in effect on shall be the initial rules of the Commission.September 30, 1994

children.heading: Cooperation
children.label: e Cooperation
children.text: All Federal agencies shall cooperate fully with the Commission to the end that it may effectively carry out its functions and duties.
